title: Information Technology Act, 2000
description: An Act to provide legal recognition for transactions carried out by means of electronic data interchange and other means of electronic communication.
maxPenalty: Up to 3 yrs &/or Rs 5 lakh (s.66); up to 5 yrs for content offences; s.43A compensation
judicialStatus: s.66A struck down (Shreya Singhal, 2015); s.69A blocking upheld

title: Information Technology (Amendment) Act, 2008
description: The major overhaul of the IT Act. It introduced critical information infrastructure and protected systems (ss.70A/70B), reasonable-security and data-protection duties (ss.43A, 72A), lawful interception, monitoring and blocking powers (ss.69, 69A, 69B), intermediary due-diligence and safe harbour (s.79), legal recognition for electronic signatures, and several new cyber offences.
maxPenalty: Penalties as inserted into the IT Act (ss.43A, 66, 66E, 67, 72A)

title: Information Technology (Certifying Authorities) Rules, 2000
description: Set the operational framework for licensed Certifying Authorities that issue digital signature certificates, covering the application and licensing process, security standards, audit and the duties of Certifying Authorities.
section: Rules / Regulations under the IT Act

title: Information Technology (Certifying Authority) Regulations, 2001
description: Lay down the licensing terms and operational, security and audit requirements for Certifying Authorities, covering identity verification, certificate and CRL management, time stamping, record keeping and suspension procedures. They also prescribe minimum cryptography and directory standards.
section: Rules / Regulations under the IT Act

title: Information Technology (Recognition of Foreign Certifying Authorities operating under Regulatory Authority) Regulations, 2013
description: Set out how the Controller of Certifying Authorities may recognise a foreign certifying authority operating under a foreign regulatory authority, so that the electronic signature certificates it issues are treated as valid in India.
section: Rules / Regulations under the IT Act

title: Information Technology (Procedure and Safeguards for Interception, Monitoring and Decryption of Information) Rules, 2009
description: Framed under section 69, these rules set the procedure and safeguards for lawful interception, monitoring and decryption of information in any computer resource. Each case needs the approval of the Union Home Secretary and is reviewed by a committee headed by the Cabinet Secretary.
section: Rules / Regulations under the IT Act

title: Information Technology (Procedure and Safeguards for Blocking for Access of Information by Public) Rules, 2009
description: Framed under section 69A, these rules set out how the government directs blocking of public access to online content, including the Designated Officer, an inter-departmental committee, provision for a hearing, emergency blocking and confidentiality of requests and actions.
section: Rules / Regulations under the IT Act

title: Information Technology (Procedure and Safeguards for Monitoring and Collecting Traffic Data or Information) Rules, 2009
description: Framed under section 69B, these rules set the procedure and safeguards for the government to monitor and collect traffic data or information from any computer resource to enhance cyber security, with CERT-In as the designated agency.
section: Rules / Regulations under the IT Act

title: Information Technology (Reasonable Security Practices and Procedures and Sensitive Personal Data or Information) Rules, 2011 (SPDI Rules)
description: Framed under section 43A, these rules defined 'sensitive personal data or information' and required body corporates handling it to follow reasonable security practices (such as ISO 27001), publish a privacy policy, obtain consent and appoint a grievance officer. They were superseded by the Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025.
section: Rules / Regulations under the IT Act
compliance: Privacy policy; ISO-27001-grade security; consent; grievance officer

title: Information Technology (Intermediaries Guidelines) Rules, 2011
description: Framed under section 79, these rules set the original due-diligence obligations for intermediaries seeking safe harbour. They were replaced by the IT (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Rules, 2021.
section: Rules / Regulations under the IT Act

title: Information Technology (Guidelines for Cyber Cafe) Rules, 2011
description: Require cyber cafes to register with a registration agency, verify and record the identity of users, retain logs of use, and follow specified physical and security conditions.
section: Rules / Regulations under the IT Act
compliance: Verify user identity; keep usage logs; register

title: Information Technology (Electronic Service Delivery) Rules, 2011
description: Enable government departments to deliver public services electronically, letting the government notify services, service providers and the manner of electronic delivery.
section: Rules / Regulations under the IT Act

title: Information Technology (The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team and Manner of Performing Functions and Duties) Rules, 2013
description: Framed under section 70B, these rules set out how CERT-In functions as the national agency for cyber-incident response, including the types of incidents that must be reported to it and the powers it may exercise.
section: Rules / Regulations under the IT Act

title: Information Technology (National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre and Manner of Performing Functions and Duties) Rules, 2013
description: Framed under section 70A, these rules designate the NCIIPC as the national nodal agency for protecting critical information infrastructure and set the security obligations of organisations that run protected systems.
section: Rules / Regulations under the IT Act

title: Electronic Signature or Electronic Authentication Technique and Procedure Rules, 2015
description: Prescribe the electronic authentication techniques and procedures recognised under the IT Act, including e-authentication using Aadhaar e-KYC (e-Sign), so that such signatures carry legal validity.
section: Rules / Regulations under the IT Act

title: Information Technology (Information Security Practices and Procedures for Protected System) Rules, 2018
description: Framed under section 70, these rules set the information-security governance that an organisation running a protected system must follow, including an Information Security Steering Committee, a designated officer, network segregation and periodic audits.
section: Rules / Regulations under the IT Act
compliance: CISO; Information Security Steering Committee; audits; network segregation

title: S.O.1015(E), 19.9.2002 - Information Technology (Removal of Difficulties) Order, 2002
description: Issued to remove difficulties that came up in giving effect to the IT Act, 2000, making the clarificatory adjustments needed in its early implementation.

title: G.S.R.181(E), 27.2.2003 - establishment of CERT-In and procedure for blocking of websites
description: Established the Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) and laid down the procedure then used for blocking websites. It was later rescinded once dedicated blocking rules and a statutory basis for CERT-In came into force.

title: G.S.R.240(E), 25.3.2003 - Secretary of State/UT IT Department as Adjudicating Officer
description: Appoints the Secretary of the IT Department of each State and Union Territory as the adjudicating officer under section 46, to decide contraventions of the IT Act in their jurisdiction.

title: Designated Officer under section 69A and Rule 3 of the IT (Blocking) Rules, 2009
description: Names the Designated Officer who can direct the blocking of public access to online information under section 69A, following the procedure in the 2009 Blocking Rules.

title: Rescinding of earlier notifications G.S.R. 181(E) and G.S.R. 529(E)
description: Withdraws the two earlier notifications dealing with CERT-In and the older website-blocking procedure, after the 2009 Blocking Rules replaced them.

title: Declaring TETRA Secured Communication System Network of Govt. of NCT of Delhi as Protected System under section 70
description: Declares the TETRA Secured Communication System Network of the Government of NCT of Delhi a protected system under section 70, so that only government-authorised persons may access it.

title: Process of registration of cyber cafe to be followed by the registration agency
description: Sets out the process a cyber cafe must follow to register with the registration agency under the IT (Guidelines for Cyber Cafe) Rules, 2011, including user-identity verification and record-keeping.

title: Constitution of Cyber Regulations Advisory Committee
description: Constitutes the Cyber Regulations Advisory Committee under section 88, which advises the Central Government and the Controller on rules and regulations made under the Act.

title: Declaration of UIDAI-CIDR critical information under section 70A of the IT Act
description: Declares the UIDAI's Central Identities Data Repository (CIDR), with its facilities, information assets, logistics infrastructure and dependencies, a protected system, recognising it as critical information infrastructure under the Act.

title: Section 69B - bringing CERT-In within scope to enhance cyber security
description: Brings CERT-In within section 69B for cyber security, so it can help identify, analyse and prevent intrusions and the spread of computer contaminants.

title: Authorisation of CERT-In to monitor and collect traffic data or information u/s 69B
description: Authorises CERT-In to monitor and collect traffic data or information from any computer resource under section 69B, for the purpose of enhancing cyber security.

title: Powers and Functions of Chairperson, Cyber Appellate Tribunal (CyAT)
description: Specifies the powers and functions of the Chairperson of the Cyber Appellate Tribunal, which heard appeals against orders of the Controller and adjudicating officers (its functions were later merged into TDSAT).

title: Central Forensic Science Laboratory, Hyderabad (DFSS, MHA) as Examiner of Electronic Evidence
description: Notifies the CFSL, Hyderabad, under the Directorate of Forensic Science Services, MHA, as an Examiner of Electronic Evidence under section 79A, to give expert opinion on electronic evidence before any court or authority.

title: Directorate of Forensic Science, Gandhi Nagar (Gujarat) as Examiner of Electronic Evidence
description: Notifies the Directorate of Forensic Science, Gandhi Nagar, as an Examiner of Electronic Evidence under section 79A for the State of Gujarat.

title: Computer Forensic and Data Mining Laboratory, SFIO (MCA) as Examiner of Electronic Evidence
description: Notifies the Computer Forensic and Data Mining Laboratory under the Serious Fraud Investigation Office, MCA, as an Examiner of Electronic Evidence under section 79A, covering computer (media) forensics but excluding floppy disk drives.

title: Forensic Science Laboratory, Sector 14, Rohini, New Delhi as Examiner of Electronic Evidence
description: Notifies the FSL, Rohini, under the Government of NCT of Delhi, as an Examiner of Electronic Evidence under section 79A.

title: Cyber Forensic Laboratory, Army Cyber Group, DGMO as Examiner of Electronic Evidence
description: Notifies the Cyber Forensic Laboratory under the Army Cyber Group, Directorate General of Military Operations, as an Examiner of Electronic Evidence under section 79A.

title: State Forensic Science Laboratory, Madiwala, Bengaluru (Karnataka) as Examiner of Electronic Evidence
description: Notifies the State FSL, Madiwala, Bengaluru, under the Karnataka Police Department, as an Examiner of Electronic Evidence under section 79A.

title: Information Technology (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Rules, 2021
description: Set out due-diligence duties for intermediaries, including grievance redressal and extra obligations for significant social media intermediaries, plus a code of ethics with a three-tier grievance mechanism for digital news publishers and OTT streaming platforms.
judicialStatus: Part III (digital-media ethics) stayed by High Courts; challenges pending before the Supreme Court
compliance: Due diligence; grievance officer; SSMI - compliance/nodal/grievance officers; monthly reports; traceability

title: Information Technology (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Amendment Rules, 2022
description: Strengthened intermediaries' due-diligence duties (including respecting users' constitutional rights and acting on certain content faster) and created the Grievance Appellate Committees to hear user appeals against intermediaries' grievance officers.

title: Information Technology (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Amendment Rules, 2023
description: Added an online-gaming framework (self-regulatory bodies to certify permissible online real-money games) and empowered a government fact-check unit to flag false or misleading information about the Central Government's business; the fact-check unit provision was later struck down by the Bombay High Court in 2024.
judicialStatus: Fact-check-unit provision struck down (Bombay HC, Kunal Kamra, 2024)
compliance: Online-gaming SRB certification; act on fact-check-unit flags

title: Cyber Forensic Division, SFSL, Vellayambalam, Thiruvananthapuram (Kerala) as Examiner of Electronic Evidence
description: Notifies the Cyber Forensic Division of the State FSL, Thiruvananthapuram, as an Examiner of Electronic Evidence under section 79A.

title: Cyber Forensic Laboratory, Air Force Cyber Group, New Delhi as Examiner of Electronic Evidence
description: Notifies the Cyber Forensic Laboratory under the Air Force Cyber Group, New Delhi, as an Examiner of Electronic Evidence under section 79A.

title: Designated Officer under section 69A of the Act
description: Names the Designated Officer empowered to issue content and website blocking directions under section 69A and the 2009 Blocking Rules.

title: HDFC Bank declared a protected system
description: Declares certain computer resources of HDFC Bank and its associated dependencies a protected system under section 70, restricting access to government-authorised persons.

title: ICICI Bank declared a protected system
description: Declares certain computer resources of ICICI Bank and its associated dependencies a protected system under section 70.

title: National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) declared a protected system
description: Declares certain computer resources of the NPCI, which runs the country's core retail payment systems, and its dependencies a protected system under section 70.

title: Licensed Telecom service providers declared protected systems
description: Declares certain computer resources of licensed telecom service providers and their associated dependencies protected systems under section 70.

title: Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) declared a protected system
description: Declares certain computer resources of LIC and its associated dependencies a protected system under section 70.

title: Amendment to the First Schedule of the IT Act
description: Amends the First Schedule, which lists documents and transactions kept outside the Act, covering certain negotiable instruments, powers of attorney, and contracts for sale or conveyance of immovable property, so they can be executed electronically.

title: Union Bank of India declared a protected system
description: Declares certain computer resources of Union Bank of India and its associated dependencies a protected system under section 70.

title: Punjab National Bank declared a protected system
description: Declares certain computer resources of Punjab National Bank and its associated dependencies a protected system under section 70.

title: Bank of Baroda declared a protected system
description: Declares certain computer resources of Bank of Baroda and its associated dependencies a protected system under section 70.

title: State Bank of India declared a protected system
description: Declares certain computer resources of the State Bank of India and its associated dependencies a protected system under section 70.

title: NIC email infrastructure declared protected systems
description: Declares the email infrastructure of the National Informatics Centre, as critical information infrastructure, and its dependencies protected systems under section 70.

title: Axis Bank declared a protected system
description: Declares certain computer resources of Axis Bank and its associated dependencies a protected system under section 70.

title: Kotak Mahindra Bank declared a protected system
description: Declares certain computer resources of Kotak Mahindra Bank and its associated dependencies a protected system under section 70.

title: Canara Bank declared a protected system
description: Declares certain computer resources of Canara Bank and its associated dependencies a protected system under section 70.

title: Establishment of three Grievance Appellate Committees (GACs) under the 2021 IT Rules
description: Sets up three Grievance Appellate Committees and appoints their members under the 2021 IT Rules. The committees hear user appeals against the decisions of intermediaries' grievance officers.

title: HDFC Bank declared a protected system (further resources)
description: Declares further computer resources of HDFC Bank and its associated dependencies a protected system under section 70.

title: ICICI Bank declared a protected system (further resources)
description: Declares further computer resources of ICICI Bank and its associated dependencies a protected system under section 70.

title: Airports Authority of India declared protected systems
description: Declares certain computer resources of the Airports Authority of India and its associated dependencies protected systems under section 70.

title: Bank of India declared protected systems
description: Declares certain computer resources of Bank of India and its associated dependencies protected systems under section 70.

title: Central Bank of India declared a protected system
description: Declares certain computer resources of the Central Bank of India and its associated dependencies a protected system under section 70.

title: Paytm Payments Bank Ltd declared a protected system
description: Declares certain computer resources of Paytm Payments Bank and its associated dependencies a protected system under section 70.

title: YES Bank declared a protected system
description: Declares certain computer resources of YES Bank and its associated dependencies a protected system under section 70.

title: IDBI Bank Ltd declared a protected system
description: Declares certain computer resources of IDBI Bank and its associated dependencies a protected system under section 70.

title: Indian Bank declared a protected system
description: Declares certain computer resources of Indian Bank and its associated dependencies a protected system under section 70.

title: All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS), New Delhi declared protected systems
description: Declares certain computer resources of AIIMS, New Delhi, and its associated dependencies protected systems under section 70.

title: National Crime Records Bureau (NCRB) declared a protected system
description: Declares certain computer resources of the NCRB and its associated dependencies a protected system under section 70.

title: Designated link officer under section 69A of the IT Act
description: Designates a link officer to act under section 69A in the blocking process, supporting the Designated Officer.

title: Kfin Technologies Private Limited declared a protected system
description: Declares certain computer resources of Kfin Technologies, a registrar and transfer agent, and its dependencies a protected system under section 70.

title: Computer Age Management Services Limited (CAMS) declared a protected system
description: Declares certain computer resources of CAMS, a mutual fund registrar and transfer agent, and its dependencies a protected system under section 70.

title: National Investigation Agency (NIA) declared a protected system
description: Declares certain computer resources of the NIA and its associated dependencies a protected system under section 70.

title: Central Registry of Securitisation Asset Reconstruction and Security Interest (CERSAI) declared a protected system
description: Declares certain computer resources of CERSAI and its associated dependencies a protected system under section 70.

title: National Cyber Security Policy (2013)
description: Aims to create a secure cyber ecosystem in the country, generate trust and confidence in IT systems and transactions in cyberspace, and thereby enhance the adoption of IT across all sectors of the economy.

title: National Cyber Security Reference Framework (NCRF)
description: A non-binding framework providing guidelines to help organisations build strong cybersecurity systems. It focuses on critical sectors such as banking, telecom, power, transportation, finance, strategic entities, government and health.

title: RBI Cyber Security Framework for Banks (2016)
description: Mandates that banks create or modify their cyber security policies, procedures and technologies to address new security concerns.
compliance: Board-approved cyber policy; SOC; report incidents to RBI

title: Data Localization Requirements (2018)
description: The RBI directive for storage of payment system data, issued under s.10(2) read with s.18 of the Payment and Settlement Systems Act, 2007, requires the entire payment data to be stored in India by Payment System Operators (PSOs).
compliance: Store payment-system data only in India

title: RBI Guidelines on Digital Payments Security Controls (2021)
description: Intended to ensure that customers can securely conduct digital payments.
compliance: Baseline security controls for digital payments

title: IRDAI Guidelines on Information and Cyber Security (2023)
description: Aim to help the insurance industry strengthen its defences and related governance mechanisms to deal with emerging cyber threats.

title: Digital Information Security in Healthcare Act (DISHA)
description: A draft framework prepared in response to a sharp rise in e-consultations, to govern the security of digital health information. It was not enacted and was largely overtaken by the ABDM and the DPDP Act.

title: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) Guidelines
description: ABDM aims to build the backbone for the country's integrated digital health infrastructure, bridging the gap among different stakeholders of the healthcare ecosystem through digital highways.

title: The Telecommunications Act, 2023
description: An Act of Parliament that replaces the Indian Telegraph Act, 1885 and the Indian Wireless Telegraphy Act, 1933, consolidating the laws on the development, expansion and operation of telecommunication services and networks.
maxPenalty: Criminal up to 3 yrs &/or Rs 2 crore; civil up to Rs 5 crore

title: Telecommunications (Administration of Digital Bharat Nidhi) Rules, 2024
description: Govern the administration of the Digital Bharat Nidhi, the fund that replaced the Universal Service Obligation Fund, setting the administrator's powers, the criteria for choosing schemes and implementers, and how money is spent on extending telecom services to underserved and remote areas and groups.

title: Telecommunication Right of Way Rules, 2024
description: Govern how facility providers obtain right of way to lay and maintain underground and overground telecom infrastructure on public and private property, through a common online portal with fixed timelines. They supersede the Indian Telegraph Right of Way Rules, 2016.

title: Telecommunications (Telecom Cyber Security) Rules, 2024
description: Require telecom entities to protect their networks and services, adopt a cyber security policy, appoint a Chief Telecommunication Security Officer, and report security incidents within fixed timelines. They also let the government collect traffic data, other than the content of messages, for cyber security.
compliance: Cyber-security policy; Chief Telecom Security Officer; incident reporting

title: Telecommunications (Critical Telecommunication Infrastructure) Rules, 2024
description: Let the government designate telecom networks whose disruption would seriously affect national security, the economy, public health or safety as Critical Telecommunication Infrastructure, and set out the security, audit, inspection and access obligations that follow.
compliance: CTI security, audit and access obligations

title: Telecommunications (Temporary Suspension of Services) Rules, 2024
description: Lay down the procedure and safeguards for ordering a temporary suspension of telecom services, including who may issue suspension orders and review by oversight committees. They replace the 2017 telecom suspension rules.

title: Telecommunications (Procedures and Safeguards for Lawful Interception of Messages) Rules, 2024
description: Set the procedure and safeguards for lawful interception of messages by authorised agencies, including approval levels, time limits and review committees. They replace Rules 419 and 419A of the Indian Telegraph Rules, 1951.
compliance: Interception only on authorised approval; records/oversight

title: Telecommunications (Commercial Radio Operator Certificate of Proficiency to Operate GMDSS) Rules, 2024
description: Govern the grant of the certificate of proficiency needed to operate the Global Maritime Distress and Safety System, covering eligibility, examination and the two GMDSS certificate categories.

title: Telecommunications (Amateur Services) Rules, 2024
description: Set the framework for amateur radio in India, including the grant of the Amateur Station Operator Certificate, eligibility, examination and operating conditions.

title: Notification of BharatNet as a special project under rule 12(1) of the RoW Rules, 2024
description: Notifies BharatNet as a special project under rule 12(1) of the Telecom Right of Way Rules, 2024, which entitles it to faster, near-automatic right of way permissions for laying its network.

title: Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) Act, 1997
description: An Act to establish the Telecom Regulatory Authority of India and the Telecom Disputes Settlement and Appellate Tribunal, to regulate telecommunication services, adjudicate disputes, dispose of appeals, protect the interests of service providers and consumers, and promote orderly growth of the telecom sector.
section: TELECOM REGULATORY AUTHORITY OF INDIA (TRAI) ACT, 1997
maxPenalty: Civil penalty for contravention of directions

title: Prohibition of Discriminatory Tariffs for Data Services Regulations, 2016
description: TRAI regulation barring service providers from offering or charging different data tariffs based on the content being accessed. It ended zero-rating platforms such as Free Basics and is a cornerstone of net neutrality in India.

title: Mobile Banking (Quality of Service) (Second Amendment) Regulations, 2016
description: Amends the Mobile Banking (Quality of Service) Regulations, 2012 to revise the framework for USSD-based mobile banking and payment services, including the number of stages allowed in a USSD session.

title: Telecom Commercial Communications Customer Preference Regulations (TCCCPR), 2018
description: TRAI's framework to curb unsolicited commercial communication. It requires senders, headers and message templates to be registered on a distributed-ledger (blockchain) platform, and lets subscribers set and control their commercial-communication preferences.
compliance: Register senders/headers/templates on DLT; honour preferences

title: Telecom Disputes Settlement and Appellate Tribunal (Officers) Recruitment Rules, 2023
description: Set the method of recruitment, qualifications and service conditions for officers of the Telecom Disputes Settlement and Appellate Tribunal.

title: The Indian Telegraph Act, 1885
description: Governs the use of wired and wireless telegraphy, telephones, teletype, radio communications and digital data communications. It is being replaced by the Telecommunications Act, 2023.
maxPenalty: Imprisonment/fine for unauthorised use of telegraphs

title: The Indian Wireless Telegraphy Act, 1933
description: Governed the possession and use of wireless telegraphy apparatus in India. It is repealed and replaced by the Telecommunications Act, 2023.

title: Order (1993): Functions of DG(T) and powers of the Telegraph Authority to be performed by Secretary (T)
description: Provides that the functions of the Director General (Telecom) and the powers of the telegraph authority under the Act shall be exercised by the Secretary (Telecom).

title: Power to Licensee - basic and cellular service providers to seek way-leave from any person, incl. Govt. bodies
description: Confers on basic and cellular service providers the telegraph authority's power to seek way-leave (right of way) from any person, including government bodies, for laying their lines.

title: Power to Licensee - basic and cellular service providers to seek way-leave from local authority
description: Confers on basic and cellular service providers the power to seek way-leave from a local authority for laying their lines.

title: Power to Licensee - MD and GM of MTNL empowered to perform some functions of the telegraph authority
description: Empowers the Managing Director and General Manager of MTNL to perform certain functions of the telegraph authority.

title: Mandatory Testing and Certification of Telecom Equipment (MTCTE) (2019)
description: A scheme that requires telecom equipment to be tested and certified before it can be sold, imported or used in India.
compliance: Test & certify telecom equipment before sale/import

title: TRAI Regulations (general)
description: TRAI issues various regulations, directions and guidelines covering a wide range of topics related to telecommunications and broadcasting.

title: The Cinematograph Act, 1952
description: An Act to provide for the certification of cinematograph films for exhibition and for regulating exhibitions by means of cinematographs.
maxPenalty: Up to 3 yrs &/or fine (incl. anti-piracy, 2023 amendment)

title: The Parliamentary Proceedings (Protection of Publication) Act, 1977
description: Protects publishers, editors and others who publish parliamentary proceedings in good faith, and prevents the use of parliamentary proceedings as evidence in court.

title: The Press Council Act, 1978
description: An Act to establish a Press Council to preserve the freedom of the Press and to maintain and improve the standards of newspapers and news agencies in India.

title: The Prasar Bharati (Broadcasting Corporation of India) Act, 1990
description: Established Prasar Bharati as India's broadcasting corporation, defining its functions, powers and composition.

title: The Cable Television Networks (Regulation) Act, 1995
description: An Act to regulate the operation of cable television networks in the country and for matters connected therewith.
maxPenalty: Up to 2 yrs &/or fine (first offence); higher for repeat

title: The Sports Broadcasting Signals (Mandatory Sharing with Prasar Bharati) Act, 2007
description: An Act to give the largest number of listeners and viewers free-to-air access to sporting events of national importance, through mandatory sharing of sports broadcasting signals with Prasar Bharati.

title: The Press and Registration of Periodicals Act, 2023
description: An Act to provide for the press, the registration of periodicals and for matters connected therewith.

title: Indian Telegraph Right of Way Rules, 2016
description: Govern the establishment, maintenance and use of telegraph lines and other telecommunication infrastructure in India, including conditions for transmitting messages, fees and dispute resolution.
section: Indian Telegraph Right of Way (RoW) Rules, 2016 (superseded by the Telecom RoW Rules, 2024)

title: Indian Telegraph Right of Way (Amendment) Rules, 2017
description: A narrow technical fix to the dispute-resolution provision (rule 14). It deleted the sixty-day window, counted from when the 2016 rules began, within which a right-of-way dispute had to be raised.
section: Indian Telegraph Right of Way (RoW) Rules, 2016 (superseded by the Telecom RoW Rules, 2024)

title: Indian Telegraph Right of Way (Amendment) Rules, 2021
description: Extended the rules, which until then covered only underground optical fibre and mobile towers, to overground (aerial) telegraph lines. It capped one-time compensation for an overground line at Rs 1,000 per km and barred any charge beyond the administrative fee and restoration charges.
section: Indian Telegraph Right of Way (RoW) Rules, 2016 (superseded by the Telecom RoW Rules, 2024)

title: Indian Telegraph Right of Way (Amendment) Rules, 2022
description: Moved all right-of-way applications onto a central electronic portal and replaced the fixed fee and compensation figures with amounts set in a new Schedule. It added rule 10A (street furniture for small cells and telegraph lines) and rule 10B (telegraph infrastructure over private property).
section: Indian Telegraph Right of Way (RoW) Rules, 2016 (superseded by the Telecom RoW Rules, 2024)

title: Indian Telegraph Right of Way Rules, 2016 - as amended in 2017, 2021 and 2022
description: Consolidated text of the 2016 rules incorporating the 2017, 2021 and 2022 amendments, the working version in use before the 2023 changes.
section: Indian Telegraph Right of Way (RoW) Rules, 2016 (superseded by the Telecom RoW Rules, 2024)

title: Indian Telegraph Right of Way (Amendment) Rules, 2023
description: Added rule 9A, letting a licensee put up temporary overground infrastructure for up to sixty days, free of charge, to restore service when its underground infrastructure is damaged. It also defined 'street furniture,' allowed a single application to cover multiple sites, waived fees for small-cell deployment on government buildings, and revised the fee Schedule.
section: Indian Telegraph Right of Way (RoW) Rules, 2016 (superseded by the Telecom RoW Rules, 2024)

title: Consolidated Indian Telegraph Right of Way Rules, 2016 - as amended in 2017, 2021, 2022, 2023
description: Consolidated text of the 2016 rules incorporating all amendments up to and including 2023.
section: Indian Telegraph Right of Way (RoW) Rules, 2016 (superseded by the Telecom RoW Rules, 2024)

title: Guidelines for Indian Government Websites (GIGW) 3.0
description: GIGW aims to ensure the quality and accessibility of government websites by offering guidance on desirable practices across the whole lifecycle of websites, portals and web applications, from conceptualisation and design to development, maintenance and management.

title: CERT-In Directions under section 70B(6), 2022
description: Directions requiring service providers, intermediaries, data centres, body corporates and government bodies to report specified cyber incidents to CERT-In within six hours, synchronise clocks to NIC/NPL time servers, keep ICT logs for 180 days in India, and (for data centres, VPS, cloud, VPN and virtual asset providers) retain KYC and transaction records for five years.
maxPenalty: Up to 1 yr &/or Rs 1 lakh (IT Act s.70B(7))
compliance: Report incidents in 6h; retain ICT logs 180d in India; KYC/records 5y; sync clocks to NIC/NPL

title: Cybersecurity and Cyber Resilience Framework (CSCRF), 2024
description: A SEBI framework that aims to address evolving cyber threats, align with industry standards, encourage efficient audits, and ensure compliance by SEBI Regulated Entities. It also sets out standard reporting formats for those entities.
compliance: Cyber-resilience controls; standard incident reporting (SEBI REs)

title: Right to Information Act, 2005
description: Empowers citizens, promotes transparency and accountability in the working of the Government, contains corruption, and helps make democracy work for the people in practice.
maxPenalty: Rs 250/day up to Rs 25,000 on the PIO + disciplinary action

title: Government Open Data License - India / National Data Sharing and Accessibility Policy
description: Grants the Government Open Data License - India, a royalty-free licence that allows users to access, use and publish non-sensitive government data.

title: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (2023)
description: Applies to the processing of digital personal data within India, including data collected in digital form and non-digital data that has been digitised. Its provisions began commencing in phases from November 2025.
maxPenalty: Up to Rs 250 crore per violation (civil only; effective 13 May 2027)
compliance: Consent notice; security safeguards; breach notice; DPO (SDF); grievance redressal

title: Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025
description: Operationalise the DPDP Act, 2023, setting the detail on notices and consent, consent managers, security safeguards, breach notification (a short first report plus a fuller one within 72 hours), children's and disabled persons' data, additional duties for Significant Data Fiduciaries, and cross-border transfer. They commence in phases up to May 2027 and repeal the SPDI Rules, 2011.
maxPenalty: Under DPDP Act (up to Rs 250 crore)
compliance: Consent manager; breach notice <=72h; verifiable parental consent; SDF audit/DPIA; cross-border rules

title: DPDP Act - enforcement / commencement notification, 2025
description: Notifies the phased commencement of the DPDP Act and Rules in three tranches - immediate procedural provisions, consent-manager provisions after one year, and the substantive obligations after eighteen months (by May 2027).

title: Establishment of the Data Protection Board of India, 2025
description: Establishes the Data Protection Board of India, the adjudicatory body under the DPDP Act, in the National Capital Region, and fixes its membership at four.

title: Draft National Data Governance Framework Policy
description: Aims to make non-personal and anonymised data from Government and private entities safely accessible to the research and innovation ecosystem, providing an institutional framework of rules, standards and protocols for sharing such data while ensuring privacy, security and trust.

title: The Aadhaar (Targeted Delivery of Financial and other Subsidies, Benefits and Services) Act, 2016
description: Provides for good governance and the efficient, transparent and targeted delivery of subsidies, benefits and services funded from the Consolidated Fund of India, by assigning unique identity numbers to individuals residing in India.
maxPenalty: Up to 3 yrs &/or Rs 10 lakh (criminal); civil penalties up to Rs 1 crore
judicialStatus: Upheld with modifications (K.S. Puttaswamy, 2018); s.57 struck down

title: The Aadhaar and Other Laws (Amendment) Act, 2019
description: Amended the Aadhaar Act and allied laws after the Supreme Court's Puttaswamy judgment. It made Aadhaar authentication for banking and telecom voluntary with alternative verification, allowed offline verification, barred storage of core biometrics by requesting entities, and added civil penalties and an adjudication mechanism.
maxPenalty: Added civil penalties up to Rs 1 crore + Rs 10 lakh/day (continuing)

title: The Aadhaar (Data Security) Regulations, 2016
description: Set the information security measures and policy that UIDAI and its ecosystem must follow to secure Aadhaar data, including the Central Identities Data Repository.
section: Rules / Regulations on the Aadhaar Act

title: The UIDAI (Transaction of Business at Meetings of the Authority) Regulations, 2016
description: Lay down how the Authority conducts its meetings, including quorum, notice and decision-making.
section: Rules / Regulations on the Aadhaar Act

title: The Aadhaar (Sharing of Information) Regulations, 2016
description: Set out when and how identity information held in the Aadhaar system may be shared, and the restrictions on requesting entities and others handling that information.
section: Rules / Regulations on the Aadhaar Act

title: The Aadhaar (Enrolment and Update) Regulations, 2016
description: Govern enrolment for Aadhaar and the updating of demographic and biometric information, including the process, documents and responsibilities involved.
section: Rules / Regulations on the Aadhaar Act

title: The Aadhaar (Payment of Fees for Performance of Authentication) Regulations, 2023
description: Set the fees that requesting entities pay UIDAI for authentication and e-KYC services.
section: Rules / Regulations on the Aadhaar Act

title: The UIDAI (Salary, Allowances and other Terms and Conditions of Service of Employees) Regulations, 2020
description: Fix the pay, allowances and service conditions of UIDAI employees.
section: Rules / Regulations on the Aadhaar Act

title: The Aadhaar (Authentication and Offline Verification) Regulations, 2021
description: Govern online Aadhaar authentication and offline verification, including the modes available and the duties of requesting and verifying entities. They replaced the Aadhaar (Authentication) Regulations, 2016.
section: Rules / Regulations on the Aadhaar Act

title: The UIDAI (Appointment of Officers and Employees) Regulations, 2020
description: Set the method of appointment, qualifications and recruitment process for UIDAI officers and employees.
section: Rules / Regulations on the Aadhaar Act

title: Establishment of the Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI)
description: Establishes UIDAI as the statutory authority under the Aadhaar Act.

title: Enforcement of sections 11 to 20, 22 to 23 and 48 to 59 of the Aadhaar Act, 2016
description: Brings into force the sections covering the establishment and functioning of UIDAI.

title: Appointment of the Chairperson and Members of UIDAI
description: Appoints the Chairperson and Members of UIDAI.

title: Enforcement of sections 1 to 10 and 24 to 47 of the Aadhaar Act, 2016
description: Brings into force the sections covering enrolment, authentication and related provisions of the Act.

title: D/o Food & PD notification under section 7 (Aadhaar requirement for NFSA foodgrains)
description: Requires beneficiaries to have Aadhaar, or an enrolment ID, to receive subsidised foodgrains or cash transfer of food subsidy under the National Food Security Act, under section 7 of the Aadhaar Act, and sets a deadline for enrolment.

title: Target date for Aadhaar enrolment under the R&D Programme in the Water Sector scheme
description: Notifies the target date for Aadhaar enrolment of beneficiaries under the Research and Development Programme in the Water Sector scheme.

title: Date for Aadhaar enrolment by Central/State organisations under the National Water Mission
description: Notifies the date for Aadhaar enrolment of organisations seeking grant-in-aid under the National Water Mission central sector scheme.

title: Target date for Aadhaar enrolment by Central/State organisations under the National Water Mission
description: Notifies the target date for the same enrolment under the National Water Mission scheme.

title: Corrigendum to the D/o Food & PD notification dated 8 Feb 2017
description: Corrects the Department of Food and Public Distribution's PDS notification of 8 February 2017 on the Aadhaar requirement.

title: Target date for Aadhaar enrolment for HRD painting and essay competitions (IEC component)
description: Notifies the target date for Aadhaar enrolment of children winning prizes under the painting and essay competitions of the HRD capacity-building scheme.

title: Extension of Aadhaar/EID submission deadline to 30 September 2017 (D/o Food & PD)
description: Extends the deadline for submitting Aadhaar or enrolment ID under the PDS notification to 30 September 2017.

title: Every Scheduled Commercial Bank to provide Aadhaar enrolment/update facilities (Reg. 12A)
description: Requires every scheduled commercial bank to offer Aadhaar enrolment and update facilities to its customers, under the 2017 enrolment regulations.

title: Extension of Aadhaar/EID submission deadline to 31 December 2017 (D/o Food & PD)
description: Extends the PDS Aadhaar/EID submission deadline to 31 December 2017.

title: Process for placing and overriding bank accounts on the Aadhaar Payment Bridge / NPCI Mapper
description: Lays down the process for placing and overriding bank accounts on the Aadhaar Payment Bridge / NPCI Mapper.

title: Extension of Aadhaar/EID submission deadline to 31 March 2018 (D/o Food & PD)
description: Extends the PDS Aadhaar/EID submission deadline to 31 March 2018.

title: Extension of Aadhaar/EID submission deadline to 30 June 2018 (D/o Food & PD)
description: Extends the PDS Aadhaar/EID submission deadline to 30 June 2018.

title: Further amendment to S.O. 371(E) dated 8 Feb 2017 (D/o Food & PD)
description: Further amends the D/o Food & Public Distribution notification S.O. 371(E), revising the deadline by which eligible NFSA beneficiaries without Aadhaar must apply for enrolment (substituted in paragraphs 1(2) and 2(1) of that order).

title: Further amendment to the notification dated 8.2.2017 (Min. of Consumer Affairs)
description: Further amends the same S.O. 371(E) notification, again revising the Aadhaar-enrolment deadline for NFSA beneficiaries.

title: Extension of Aadhaar/EID submission deadline to 31 March 2019 (D/o Food & PD)
description: Extends the PDS Aadhaar/EID submission deadline to 31 March 2019.

title: Further amendment to S.O. 371(E) dated 8 Feb 2017 (D/o Food & PD)
description: Further amends the same S.O. 371(E) notification, revising the Aadhaar-enrolment deadline and related publicity provisions for NFSA beneficiaries.

title: Further amendment to S.O. 371(E) dated 8 Feb 2017 (D/o Food & PD)
description: Further amends the same S.O. 371(E) notification, again revising the Aadhaar-enrolment deadline for NFSA beneficiaries.

title: Commencement of sections 1 to 30 of the Aadhaar and Other Laws (Amendment) Act, 2019
description: Notifies the date from which sections 1 to 30 of the Aadhaar and Other Laws (Amendment) Act, 2019 come into force.
maxPenalty: Added civil penalties up to Rs 1 crore + Rs 10 lakh/day (continuing)

title: NRIs entitled to obtain an Aadhaar number
description: Clarifies that non-resident Indians are entitled to obtain an Aadhaar number.

title: Extension of Aadhaar/EID submission deadline to 31 December 2019 (D/o Food & PD)
description: Extends the PDS Aadhaar/EID submission deadline to 31 December 2019.

title: Extension of the date in S.O. 371(E) dated 8 Feb 2017 (D/o Food & PD)
description: Extends the date set in the Department of Food and Public Distribution's PDS notification of 8 February 2017.

title: Proof of possession of Aadhaar number
description: Notifies the documents accepted as proof of possession of an Aadhaar number.

title: Use of Aadhaar authentication for Parichay (eKYC and verification of SSO users for G2G and G2C apps)
description: Permits the use of Aadhaar authentication for Parichay, the single sign-on platform for G2G and G2C applications, for eKYC and user verification.

title: NIXI permitted to perform Aadhaar authentication (voluntary) in domain booking
description: Allows the National Internet Exchange of India, a not-for-profit under section 8 of the Companies Act, to use Aadhaar authentication, on a voluntary basis, in the domain booking process.

title: C-DAC permitted to use Aadhaar authentication (voluntary) for e-Pramaan SSO
description: Allows C-DAC to use Aadhaar authentication, on a voluntary basis, for verification and eKYC of residents accessing government services through the e-Pramaan single sign-on.

title: Electronics and Information Technology Goods (Requirements for Compulsory Registration) Order, 2012
description: Mandates that certain electronic and information technology goods meet Indian safety standards and be registered with the Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS).
compliance: Compulsory BIS registration for notified goods

title: Gazette notification of the E-mail Policy of the Government of India
description: Notifies the email policy governing use of the government's email service by its departments and employees.

title: Gazette notification of the Policy on Use of IT Resources of the Government of India
description: Notifies the policy governing how government departments and employees may use official IT resources.

title: e-Waste (Management and Handling) Rules
description: Ensure that e-waste is handled and recycled in an environmentally sound way, to protect health and the environment from its harmful effects.
compliance: Extended producer responsibility; channel e-waste to authorised recyclers

title: Notification of the Digital Locker Rules
description: Notifies the rules for DigiLocker, the platform for issuing and storing official documents and certificates in digital form.

title: Notification of the DigiDhan Mission
description: Notifies the DigiDhan Mission, set up to promote and expand digital payments across the country.

title: Extension of the DigiDhan Mission
description: Extends the DigiDhan Mission for a further period.

title: Semiconductor Integrated Circuits Layout-Design Act, 2000
description: Provides intellectual-property protection for original layout designs of semiconductor integrated circuits, through a registration regime, exclusive rights for the registered proprietor and remedies against infringement.
maxPenalty: Up to 3 yrs &/or Rs 50,000-Rs 10 lakh (layout-design infringement)

title: India Semiconductor Mission (Semicon India Programme), 2021
description: The umbrella programme to build a domestic semiconductor and display ecosystem, offering fiscal support for fabs, display fabs and compound-semiconductor/ATMP units, and for chip design through the Design-Linked Incentive scheme, run by the India Semiconductor Mission.

title: The Promotion and Regulation of Online Gaming Act, 2025
description: A central law for online gaming. It bans online money games (whether based on skill or chance) along with their advertising and financing, promotes e-sports and online social games, and provides for an Online Gaming Authority, with blocking of non-compliant platforms under the IT Act and criminal penalties.
maxPenalty: Up to 3 yrs &/or Rs 1 crore (offer/finance); 2 yrs/Rs 50 lakh (ads); higher for repeat
compliance: Prohibition compliance; comply with blocking/Authority directions

title: The Promotion and Regulation of Online Gaming Rules, 2025
description: Rules under the Online Gaming Act that set the detail for recognising and registering e-sports and online social games, the working of the Online Gaming Authority, and enforcement.
compliance: Register e-sports / online social games with the Authority

title: Consumer Protection (E-Commerce) Rules, 2020
description: Framed under the Consumer Protection Act, 2019, these rules govern e-commerce entities (both marketplace and inventory models), requiring disclosures about sellers and goods, a grievance-redressal mechanism, and barring unfair trade practices and manipulation of prices or reviews.
compliance: Seller/goods disclosures; grievance officer; no unfair practices

title: The Payment and Settlement Systems Act, 2007
description: Gives the Reserve Bank of India the authority to regulate and supervise payment systems in India, including digital and card payments and the operators that run them. It is the parent law for the RBI's payment-data storage (localisation) directive.
maxPenalty: Fine up to Rs 10 lakh + daily fine; imprisonment for specified offences

title: Guidelines for Acquiring and Producing Geospatial Data and Geospatial Data Services, 2021
description: Liberalised the geospatial sector by removing prior-approval requirements for Indian entities to acquire, produce and use maps and geospatial data, subject to a negative list of sensitive attributes.

title: National Geospatial Policy, 2022
description: Sets a whole-of-government framework to develop India's geospatial ecosystem, with targets, institutional structure and support for an integrated data infrastructure.

title: National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence (#AIforAll), 2018
description: NITI Aayog's strategy paper setting India's approach to AI, prioritising five sectors (health, agriculture, education, smart cities and mobility) and the goal of using AI for inclusive growth.

title: Principles for Responsible AI, 2021
description: NITI Aayog's approach documents setting out principles for responsible AI - safety and reliability, equality, inclusivity, privacy, transparency and accountability - to guide ethical AI adoption.

title: MeitY Advisory on Artificial Intelligence, 2024
description: An advisory to intermediaries and platforms on deploying AI and generative models responsibly, including labelling of synthetically generated content; it was issued, criticised and then revised in March 2024.

title: IndiaAI Mission, 2024
description: A national mission to build India's AI stack - subsidised GPU compute, a common computing facility, curated datasets (AIKosh), foundation-model support, skilling and safe-AI institutions.

title: India AI Governance Guidelines, 2025
description: MeitY's framework for governing AI through a light-touch, pro-innovation approach built on seven 'sutras,' relying on existing laws and voluntary safeguards, and setting up an AI Governance Group, a Technology and Policy Expert Committee, and an AI Safety Institute.

title: National Quantum Mission (NQM), 2023
description: An eight-year mission (2023-31) to build India's quantum capability across four areas - computing, communication, sensing and metrology, and materials and devices - through thematic hubs, targeting quantum computers of 50-1000 qubits and 2,000 km quantum-secure communication.

title: National Supercomputing Mission, 2015
description: A mission to build and deploy indigenous supercomputers (the PARAM series) and a national high-performance-computing grid for research, weather modelling and AI.

title: National Strategy on Blockchain / National Blockchain Framework, 2021
description: MeitY's strategy and framework for adopting blockchain (distributed ledger technology) in government services, setting standards, shared national infrastructure and priority use-cases.

title: GI Cloud (MeghRaj) Policy, 2014
description: The Government of India's cloud-computing policy, creating the national 'MeghRaj' cloud to host government applications and data securely and at scale.

title: Indian Space Policy, 2023
description: An executive policy liberalising the space sector: it lets private Non-Government Entities carry out end-to-end space activities, refocuses ISRO on R&D, makes IN-SPACe the single-window authoriser, and tasks NSIL with commercialisation.
international: Outer Space Treaty; Liability & Registration Conv.

title: IN-SPACe Norms, Guidelines and Procedures (NGP) for Authorisation of Space Activities, 2024
description: IN-SPACe's operating rules under the 2023 policy: the list of space activities needing authorisation, the eligibility criteria and the application process for Indian entities and, through them, foreign operators.
compliance: Obtain IN-SPACe authorisation; report change of control <=48h
international: Outer Space Treaty; Liability & Registration Conv.

title: Draft Space Activities Bill, 2017
description: A draft law to give a statutory basis to licensing and regulation of space activities, liability and registration of space objects. It lapsed and has not been enacted; the 2023 policy fills the gap without a statute.
international: Outer Space Treaty; Liability & Registration Conv.

title: FDI reform for the space sector, 2024
description: An amendment to the FDI policy easing foreign investment in space - up to 100% for satellite-component manufacturing and defined caps for satellites and launch vehicles under the automatic route.

title: The Biological Diversity Act, 2002
description: Gives effect to the Convention on Biological Diversity: it regulates access to India's biological resources and associated knowledge, provides for fair and equitable benefit-sharing, and sets up the National Biodiversity Authority.
compliance: ABS approval; NBA/SBB intimation for bioresource use

title: The Biological Diversity (Amendment) Act, 2023
description: Amends the 2002 Act to ease compliance for AYUSH and research users, encourage investment and patents on Indian bioresources, decriminalise offences (replacing them with monetary penalties), and refine benefit-sharing.
maxPenalty: Rs 1 lakh-Rs 50 lakh (civil, post-2023; higher for continuing)

title: The Biological Diversity Rules, 2024
description: Rules operationalising the amended Biological Diversity Act, including the working of the authorities, access and benefit-sharing procedures, and the penalty and adjudication mechanism.

title: Manufacture, Use, Import, Export and Storage of Hazardous Micro-organisms / Genetically Engineered Organisms or Cells Rules, 1989
description: Framed under the Environment (Protection) Act, these are India's core biosafety rules governing GMOs and genetically engineered organisms, administered through the Genetic Engineering Appraisal Committee (GEAC) and allied committees.

title: BioE3 Policy (Biotechnology for Economy, Environment and Employment), 2024
description: A policy to make India a hub for high-performance biomanufacturing, supporting biotech innovation, biofoundries and bio-AI hubs across themes such as bio-based chemicals, precision biotherapeutics, climate-resilient agriculture, and marine and space biotech.

title: DNA Technology (Use and Application) Regulation Bill, 2019
description: A draft law to regulate the use of DNA technology for identification in criminal, civil and identification matters, and to set up DNA data banks and a regulatory board. It lapsed and was withdrawn in 2023.

title: The Atomic Energy Act, 1962
description: The framework law giving the Central Government control over atomic energy and nuclear materials - their development, use, production, and the mining and handling of prescribed substances - and creating a largely state-run nuclear sector.

title: The Civil Liability for Nuclear Damage Act, 2010
description: Establishes a no-fault civil liability regime for nuclear damage, channelling primary liability to the operator with a limited right of recourse against suppliers, and provides for compensation and claims adjudication.
maxPenalty: Operator liability capped at Rs 1,500 crore (civil, no-fault)

title: The Foreign Trade (Development and Regulation) Act, 1992
description: The parent law for regulating India's foreign trade. It empowers the government to make the Foreign Trade Policy, appoint the DGFT, and control imports and exports - including the dual-use export-control (SCOMET) regime.
maxPenalty: Penalty up to 5x value of goods + confiscation
compliance: Comply with FTP; hold IEC; observe export/import controls

title: Weapons of Mass Destruction and their Delivery Systems (Prohibition of Unlawful Activities) Act, 2005
description: Prohibits unlawful activities - manufacture, transport, transfer and financing - relating to weapons of mass destruction (nuclear, biological, chemical) and their delivery systems, giving effect to India's non-proliferation obligations.
maxPenalty: Imprisonment (up to life for grave offences) & fine

title: SCOMET List and dual-use export-control regime
description: The Special Chemicals, Organisms, Materials, Equipment and Technologies list controls the export of dual-use and strategic items and technologies. Administered by the DGFT under the Foreign Trade Policy, it is India's main dual-use export-control instrument.
compliance: Export authorisation & end-use compliance for controlled items

title: The Mines and Minerals (Development and Regulation) Act, 1957
description: The framework law for the mining sector - grant of mineral concessions, royalties, and the respective roles of the Centre and States. It is the vehicle for India's critical-minerals reforms.
maxPenalty: Up to 5 yrs &/or Rs 5 lakh per hectare (illegal mining, s.21)

title: The Mines and Minerals (Development and Regulation) Amendment Act, 2023
description: Created a list of 24 critical and strategic minerals, moved six (including lithium) out of the atomic-minerals list to open them to private mining, empowered the Centre to exclusively auction their concessions, and introduced exploration licences for deep-seated minerals.
maxPenalty: As per MMDR Act (up to 5 yrs / Rs 5 lakh per ha)

title: The Mines and Minerals (Development and Regulation) Amendment Act, 2025
description: Further amends the 1957 Act - renaming and widening the National Mineral Exploration Trust to also fund development and offshore/overseas exploration, removing the sale cap on captive mines, and easing addition of critical minerals to existing leases.
maxPenalty: As per MMDR Act (up to 5 yrs / Rs 5 lakh per ha)

title: The Offshore Areas Mineral (Development and Regulation) Act, 2002
description: Provides for the development and regulation of mineral resources in India's offshore areas - territorial waters, continental shelf and exclusive economic zone - including production leases and the administering authority.

title: The Offshore Areas Mineral (Development and Regulation) Amendment Act, 2023
description: Amends the 2002 offshore-minerals law to enable auction-based grant of offshore mineral blocks (including polymetallic nodules and critical-mineral-bearing blocks), paving the way for India's first offshore auction in 2024.

title: National Critical Mineral Mission (NCMM), 2025
description: A seven-year mission to secure India's critical-mineral supply chains end to end - exploration, mining, beneficiation, processing, recycling and overseas acquisition - to cut import dependence for minerals such as lithium, cobalt and rare earths.

title: The Drone Rules, 2021
description: Liberalised rules for unmanned aircraft systems - a Digital Sky platform, airspace zones (green/yellow/red), simplified registration and type certification, and eased operating requirements. They replaced the UAS Rules, 2021.
compliance: UAS registration; type certification; Digital Sky; airspace-zone compliance

title: The Bureau of Indian Standards Act, 2016
description: Makes the Bureau of Indian Standards the national standards body, with powers to set standards, run conformity-assessment and certification schemes, and enforce compulsory certification for notified goods, including many electronics and IT products.
maxPenalty: Up to 2 yrs &/or fine >= Rs 2 lakh (first offence)

title: The Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF) Act, 2023
description: Establishes the Anusandhan National Research Foundation as India's apex body to fund, coordinate and promote research and development across science, technology and innovation, drawing government and private funding and subsuming the earlier SERB.
